$schema: mlr-app exhaustive data model + permissions reference — ground truth extracted by reading all 158 files in supabase/migrations/*.sql directly (not summarized from prose docs). Companion to docs/ARCHITECTURE.json, which stays at the feature-map level; THIS file is where every column, every RLS predicate, and every RPC signature actually live.

meta.method: 8 parallel Explore-style passes each read a contiguous slice of supabase/migrations/*.sql in order (chunks: 0001-0022, 0022-0040, 0041-0059, 0060-0079, 0080-0099, 0100-0116, 0117-0136, 0137-0154) and reported every CREATE/ALTER TABLE, every CREATE POLICY (verbatim USING/WITH CHECK), every CREATE FUNCTION (full signature + body gate logic), and every GRANT/REVOKE. This file synthesizes those 8 passes into final/current state per table and function — migrations are cumulative (many tables/functions are touched 3-6 times across the range via ALTER/DROP+CREATE POLICY/CREATE OR REPLACE), and the entry below reflects the LAST migration that touched each object, in filename order (0001 -> 0154), not the original.
caveats: 158 migration files exist for numbers 0001-0154 because several numbers were used twice by different files (e.g. two files both named 0034_*, two named 0058_*, two named 0108_*) — application order for same-numbered files was not independently re-verified beyond filename lexical order; a handful of column/policy states could theoretically be swapped if the actual apply order differed. Flagged inline where a same-number collision occurred.

Some functions are 'plain SQL' (language sql) with no explicit SECURITY DEFINER/INVOKER keyword shown in the source snippet an agent captured — these default to INVOKER per Postgres semantics; called out as 'INVOKER (implicit)' below rather than assumed DEFINER.

This file does not re-verify against the live Supabase project — it is a static read of the migration files in this git checkout. If a migration was edited/hotfixed directly in the SQL editor without a matching file update, this file would miss that.

'Client-writable columns' on profiles/etc. are derived from explicit GRANT UPDATE (col list) statements found; a column's absence from that list does not necessarily mean it's unwritable if a later, unread GRANT exists outside the observed migrations — cross-check against lib/*.ts write calls if in doubt.

globalPatterns.adminCheck: Inlined everywhere (no single is_admin() helper exists): `exists (select 1 from public.profiles p where p.id = auth.uid() and p.is_admin)`. Every SECURITY DEFINER function/RLS policy that says 'admin-only' below uses this exact shape unless noted.
coreAccessPredicates: is_committee_member(cid) — admin OR committee_roster row (via committees.slug) linked_user_id = auth.uid(). (Redefined in 0057 to key off the roster, not committee_members.)
is_committee_lead(cid) — admin OR committee_members row for cid with role='Lead'.

can_access_committee_area(cid, area) — admin OR (roster-linked member AND (area IS NULL i.e. General, OR caller holds that role/'role · Lead' in committee_roster.roles)). Foundational for chat, meetings, chat_polls, search.
is_house_member(hid) — admin OR profiles.house_id = hid. Foundational for house chat, house-scoped work items, house_stays, meetings(house scope), chat_polls(house scope), search.
can_edit_fest() — admin OR committee_roster row for slug='family-fest' with linked_user_id = auth.uid(). Gates all fest_* content tables.
is_cabin_approver(cabin_id) — admin OR cabins.approver_user_id = auth.uid(). Gates cabin request review/edit/cancel/room-assign/messaging for a specific place.

can_organize_meeting(scope, committee_id, area, house_id) — admin (any scope) OR committee Lead (roster role ends '· Lead') for committee scope. House and family scopes are admin-only (no Lead concept).

_can_manage_schedule_signups(item) / _can_manage_activity_signups(act) — can_edit_fest() OR item.lead_user_id = auth.uid() OR auth.uid() = any(item.crew_user_ids). The 0110 self-edit predicate, reused by sign-ups (0135/0138) and tournaments (is_tournament_manager).

is_private_activity_member(activity) / is_private_activity_host(activity) — creator OR admin OR roster row (host role required for the 'host' variant). Private-activity-specific, mirrors the houses/cabin-approver pattern.

is_tournament_manager(t) — resolves whether the tournament hangs off a fest schedule item or a private activity, then defers to the matching predicate above. No separate tournament-organizer role exists.
globalPatterns.writeDoctrine: Every business-rule write goes through a SECURITY DEFINER RPC that re-derives its own gate (never trusts RLS alone for INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE authorization on shared tables). Plain own-row tables (reactions, home_callout_completions, apns/push_subscriptions, committee/house/committee_area reads, house rules-via-RPC) allow direct client writes gated purely by `user_id = auth.uid()` RLS. Two tables (`content_embeddings`, `chat_poll_votes`) are 'deny-all': RLS enabled, ZERO policies of any kind — not even the owner can SELECT their own row directly; access exists ONLY through a SECURITY DEFINER RPC that re-derives visibility as the caller.
escalationGuards: profiles.is_admin: never in any client UPDATE GRANT column list; INSERT policy additionally pins `is_admin = false`; only set_admin(target,value) RPC (admin-only, can't remove own admin) can change it.
profiles.house_id: never in any client UPDATE GRANT column list; only set_member_house(target,hid) RPC (admin-only) can change it.

committee_roster.linked_user_id: never directly client-writable; changes only via the bidirectional auto-link triggers (email match) or admin-driven RPCs (review_join_request, set_committee_areas, etc.).

admin_override / admin_override_votes: zero RLS policies (deny-all); a two-admin-vote 'break glass' RPC flow (request_admin_override/admin_override_status/cancel_admin_override) is the only path to a temporary is_override_unlocked() window, itself only consumable by the mac-mini via a service_role-only RPC.

columns: id uuid PK -> auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
email_alerts boolean DEFAULT true — gates ONLY the broadcast alerts/notifications email channel (alert_recipients), never transactional email (meetings/cabin-messages override it per 0132/0133)
is_admin boolean DEFAULT false — NEVER CLIENT-WRITABLE, only via set_admin() RPC
contact_email text (the account-linking key for committee_roster/family_roster)
push_level text DEFAULT 'off' — legacy (all|mentions|alerts|off), superseded by push_types but column retained
notif_types text[] DEFAULT (a long, incrementally-appended list — see functions.notifTypesEvolution) — every notification kind is opt-out per member here
intro_seen boolean DEFAULT false — false for brand-new signups, backfilled true for pre-existing members at migration time
joined_at timestamptz — NULL means an unverified/abandoned OTP signup stub; set on first email verification
house_id uuid -> houses(id) ON DELETE SET NULL — NEVER CLIENT-WRITABLE, only via set_member_house() RPC
invited_via text — tags accounts created via the admin invite-link flow, e.g. 'invite_link'
beta_tester boolean — ADDED 0029, DROPPED 0100 (concept fully removed; column no longer exists)
profiles.clientWritableColumns: display_name, avatar_url, full_name, household, bio, email_alerts, include_in_directory, phone, contact_email, venmo, zelle, cashapp, paypal, apple_cash, pay_preferred, contact_preferred, push_level, push_types, push_self_notify, push_prompted, birthday, address, notif_types, willing_to_help, intro_seen, mjt_dues_paid_year — all via column-level GRANT UPDATE, still scoped to own row by the 'profiles: update own' RLS policy.
profiles.neverClientWritable: id, is_admin, house_id, joined_at, invited_via, notify_new_members, created_at, updated_at
rls: SELECT "profiles: member read" USING (auth.uid() is not null) — tightened 0081 from an original public-read policy
INSERT "profiles: insert own" WITH CHECK (auth.uid() = id AND is_admin = false) — the 0010 escalation guard
UPDATE "profiles: update own" USING (auth.uid() = id) — column-restricted entirely via GRANT, not a narrower USING clause
profiles.notes: handle_new_user() (auth.users AFTER INSERT trigger) seeds display_name/contact_email/invited_via, but SKIPS creating a profile row entirely if email_confirmed_at is null (OTP not yet verified) — handle_user_email_confirmed() (AFTER UPDATE on auth.users) creates/upserts the row on first verification instead, stamping joined_at.

columns: display_name — FIRST NAME ONLY: split_part(trim(coalesce(nullif(display_name,''), full_name, '')), ' ', 1), masked server-side for guests
public_profiles.definition: created WITH (security_invoker = off) — runs as the view owner, deliberately bypassing profiles RLS so guests can see a masked directory without a sign-in-gated row.
public_profiles.grants: revoke all from public/anon/authenticated, then GRANT SELECT ONLY to anon AND authenticated (select-only; write-through stripped by the revoke-then-grant pattern).

push_subscriptions.notes: Mini's push-sender uses the service-role key, which bypasses RLS entirely.

rls: SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE all scoped to `user_id = auth.uid()`, role authenticated

columns: id int PK DEFAULT 1 CHECK (id=1) — singleton
admin_override.rls: enabled, ZERO policies — deny-all to every client role; only SECURITY DEFINER RPCs (request_admin_override/admin_override_status/cancel_admin_override, all admin-gated) and service_role touch it.

admin_override_votes.rls: enabled, ZERO policies — same deny-all doctrine as admin_override.

columns: image_path text (PHOTO, legacy single-image field, pre-post_media)
occurred_at timestamptz DEFAULT now() (user-editable 'when this happened' date, distinct from created_at)
status text DEFAULT 'visible' CHECK (visible|pending|hidden) — moderation state, 0040
rls: SELECT "posts: member read" USING (auth.uid() is not null AND (status='visible' OR author_id=auth.uid() OR admin)) — final form per 0081+0040 combined
INSERT "posts: insert own" WITH CHECK (auth.uid() = author_id)
UPDATE "posts: update own or admin" USING/CHECK (author_id=auth.uid() OR admin)
DELETE "posts: delete own or admin" USING (author_id=auth.uid() OR admin)
posts.notes: create_post() RPC (0080) is the real write path — atomically inserts the post + post_media rows + post_tags rows in one transaction so a partial post never lands in the feed; p_held mirrors client-side text moderation to land pending vs visible.

rls: SELECT "media: member read" USING (auth.uid() is not null) — tightened 0081
INSERT/DELETE scoped to the post's own author or admin
post_media.notes: Trigger hold_post_on_flagged_media() (0043, bugfixed 0128) sets the parent post to status='pending' when an async media_moderation verdict flags this row's storage_path.

rls: SELECT member+status-aware (0081+0040 combined, same shape as posts)
DELETE by poster, taggee, or admin
SELECT "cmsg: member read" USING (can_access_committee_area(committee_id, area) AND (status='visible' OR author_id=auth.uid() OR admin)) — final form, 0063+0128
INSERT "cmsg: member insert own" WITH CHECK (author_id=auth.uid() AND can_access_committee_area(committee_id,area) AND NOT is_committee_area_archived(committee_id,area)) — final form, 0063+0112
UPDATE "cmsg: author edit/delete 24h or admin" USING/CHECK ((author_id=auth.uid() AND created_at > now()-24h) OR admin) — 0023
SELECT scoped to can_access_committee_area via parent message (0063)
INSERT WITH CHECK — author of the message AND (mentioned user is a committee_members row for this committee OR is admin) (0024)

columns: area text DEFAULT '' (empty stands in for General/NULL)

rls: SELECT "hmsg: member read" USING (is_house_member(house_id) AND (status='visible' OR author_id=auth.uid() OR admin)) — final form 0065+0128
house_messages.notes: 1:1 mirror of committee_messages minus area sub-scoping.

rls: INSERT WITH CHECK — author AND (mentioned user shares the house OR is admin)
SELECT room-scoped via can_access_committee_area / is_house_member (0149) — no client write policy, RPC-only

chat_poll_votes.rls: ENABLED, ZERO POLICIES — confirmed deny-all (matches content_embeddings' doctrine). Not even the voter can SELECT their own row directly. NOT added to the realtime publication (nothing safe to broadcast). All access via fetch_chat_polls_for_room() (own votes only) and chat_poll_voters() (returns [] if the poll is anonymous).

rls: SELECT public read (true) — never tightened, deliberately public per 0081's own header
SELECT is_committee_member(committee_id) — no client insert/update/delete, RPC-only
SELECT self or lead (0015: `user_id=auth.uid() or is_committee_lead(committee_id)`)

columns: roles text[] DEFAULT '{}' (a trailing ' · Lead' marks a lead)
linked_user_id uuid -> profiles set null (bidirectional auto-link, 0056/0060)
rls: SELECT member-only (`auth.uid() is not null`) — tightened 0081 from public read

committee_areas.rls: no explicit `enable row level security`/policy statement found in any read chunk — flagged as unclear whether RLS was ever turned on for this table (worth confirming directly against the live project); CLAUDE.md describes it as public-read like committees.

columns: rules text DEFAULT '' (0072, up to 20000 chars, last-write-wins via set_house_rules RPC)
rls: SELECT member-only (`auth.uid() is not null`) — tightened 0081 from public read

columns: guest_names text[] DEFAULT '{}' (account-less free list, cleaned via _clean_guest_names — trim/cap 80 chars/max 40 entries)
rls: SELECT is_house_member(house_id) — no client write, RPC-only

columns: house_id uuid -> houses cascade (0066 — NULL = MLR-wide, even for guests)
rls: SELECT "work_items: member scoped read" USING (auth.uid() is not null AND (house_id is null OR is_house_member(house_id))) — final form 0066+0081 (the MLR branch was public until 0081 tightened it)

columns: event_id text (NOT an FK — supports seed/synthesized events)
rls: SELECT public read (true) — never tightened; not in 0081's members-only list

columns: media_type text DEFAULT 'image' CHECK (image|video — NO sticker/gif/file, simpler shape than chat media)
rls: SELECT scoped via parent item's house visibility
INSERT/DELETE via add_work_item_media/remove_work_item_media RPCs (item's created_by OR admin)
SELECT scoped via parent item's house visibility
SELECT/INSERT scoped — mentioned user must share the item's house scope or be admin
SELECT public read (true) — never tightened by 0081

columns: booked_by uuid -> profiles set null (0087, admin-on-behalf-of)
rls: SELECT "own or is_cabin_approver" USING (user_id=auth.uid() OR is_cabin_approver(cabin_id)) — final form 0114, widened from admin-only
SELECT own-booking-or-is_cabin_approver, via join to cabin_bookings — final form 0114

columns: body text (2000-char cap enforced in RPC)
rls: SELECT is_cabin_approver(cabin_id) only — no client write, send_cabin_message() RPC only
SELECT members-only — no client write, claim_help_item() RPC only
SELECT admin-only — no client insert policy, report_content() RPC only
SELECT admin-only — no client write, trigger/RPC only

columns: storage_path text PK (== post_media/chat media's storage_path)
media_moderation.rls: enabled, ZERO policies — deny-all; only service-role (the mini's AI check) and triggers touch it.

columns: show_banner boolean DEFAULT true (0126 — lets a send email without painting the banner)
rls: SELECT public read (true) — deliberately public per 0081's own header
INSERT posters only (can_post_alerts() — narrowed to admin-only in 0016)

columns: expires_at timestamptz (drops from badge, stays in list)
rls: NO INSERT/UPDATE policy at all — every row is written by a trigger or _notify()/send_broadcast_notification() RPC, never a raw client insert

scheduled_broadcasts.notes: Fired by pg_cron job run_scheduled_broadcasts() every minute, wrapped per-row in exception handling so one bad payload can't sink the queue.

rls: SELECT members-only — no client write, RPC-only

columns: PK (poll_id,user_id) — enforces exactly one vote per member per poll

rls: SELECT room-scoped: committee->can_access_committee_area, house->is_house_member, family(0122)->auth.uid() is not null. No client write policy anywhere — every mutation is a SECURITY DEFINER RPC.

columns: ends_at timestamptz (0122 — non-null makes it a date-range slot)
signup_enabled boolean DEFAULT false, signup_capacity int, signup_slot_minutes int, signup_start_time/end_time text (0135)
signup_mode text DEFAULT 'interval' CHECK (interval|slots|headcount — headcount added 0143), signup_instructions text, signup_fields jsonb DEFAULT '[]' (0136)
source_activity_id uuid (0141, provenance from the fest_activities merge, no FK)
links jsonb DEFAULT '[]' — ordered [{href,label}] (0142, REPLACES the earlier single link_url/link_label pair which was itself added+dropped within 0134/0142)
image_url text (PHOTO — cover image, 0134, still present; NOT replaced by 0142, which only touched the link fields)
signup_team_size int (0143 — null/1=individual, e.g. 2 for pair sign-ups)
rls: ALL can_edit_fest() write (blanket, from an earlier chunk not directly re-read but referenced throughout)

UPDATE "fest_schedule_items: lead or crew self-edit" USING/CHECK (lead_user_id=auth.uid() OR auth.uid()=any(crew_user_ids)) (0110) — a SECOND, narrower permissive policy layered on top of the blanket ALL policy; Postgres ORs them, so a lead/crew member who isn't on the Family Fest committee can still UPDATE the row at the RLS layer (the app's narrower updateScheduleDetails() call is what actually restricts which columns get submitted, not this policy).
fest_schedule_items.notes: OBJECT DIMENSIONS ANSWER: a schedule event/activity carries exactly ONE optional cover photo (image_url, plain text URL into the public site-assets bucket) and a MULTI-LINK array (links jsonb, [{href,label}] — NOT a single link) as of migration 0142. Earlier (0134) it had a single link_url/link_label pair; that was migrated into links[0] and dropped.

columns: day text (ISO date, null=event's own day)
capacity int (null falls back to item's signup_capacity)
slot_start text (interval mode; NULLABLE since 0143, was buggy not-null before)
user_id uuid -> profiles set null (null=account-less typed name)
team_id uuid (0143, shared by every row of one team sign-up)
rls: SELECT public read — no client write, sign_up_for_schedule_slot()/remove_schedule_signup() RPCs only
UPDATE "fest_dinners: chef or crew self-edit" USING/CHECK (chef_user_id=auth.uid() OR auth.uid()=any(crew_user_ids)) (0099) — same OR-layered pattern as schedule items

columns: lead_user_id/lead_name/lead_phone/crew_user_ids (0110, first lead/crew concept added — this table originally had NONE)
rls: UPDATE lead-or-crew self-edit (0110), same OR-layered pattern
fest_activities.notes: ⚠️ LEGACY as of 0141 (web retired the standalone 'anytime activities' concept in favor of anytime-flagged fest_schedule_items) — table + rows deliberately LEFT IN PLACE, not dropped, because iOS still reads it. A web edit to a converted event will NOT reflect back onto the untouched fest_activities row.

fest_activity_slots.notes: Parallel, fully isolated implementation of fest_schedule_slots for the legacy activities path (0138 mirrors 0135/0136).

fest_signup_reminders_sent.rls: enabled, ZERO policies — cron/owner-only dedupe ledger.

columns: schedule_item_id uuid -> fest_schedule_items cascade (made NULLABLE in 0150)
private_activity_id uuid -> private_activities cascade (0150, polymorphic host)
rls: SELECT "tournaments: member read" USING (auth.uid() is not null AND (private_activity_id is null OR is_private_activity_member(private_activity_id))) — final form 0150. A fest-activity tournament stays members-only as before; a private-activity tournament is visible ONLY to that activity's roster.
no client insert/update/delete — every mutation is a SECURITY DEFINER RPC (create/update/delete_tournament, generate_bracket/round_robin/pools, record_match_result, etc.)
tournaments.notes: UNIQUE INDEX tournaments_one_per_activity on (private_activity_id) where not null (0151) + idempotent create_activity_tournament() — a repeated 'Create tournament' tap can never stack duplicates.

rls: SELECT member-read, scoped through parent tournament's private_activity_id visibility (0150)

columns: entrant_id uuid -> tournament_entrants set null (NULL = the pre-team pool)
user_id uuid -> profiles set null (NULL = account-less typed name)
UNIQUE (tournament_id,user_id) where user_id not null — one app user can't be entered twice
rls: SELECT member-read, scoped via parent tournament (0150)
SELECT member-read, scoped via parent tournament (0150)
SELECT is_private_activity_member(id) — no client write, RPC-only

columns: user_id uuid -> profiles set null (null=typed name)

content_embeddings.rls: ENABLED, ZERO POLICIES — confirmed deny-all; `revoke all from anon,authenticated` + `grant all to service_role` explicitly. Only reachable via the search_conversations() SECURITY DEFINER RPC (re-derives caller-specific visibility) or the mini's service-role indexer directly.
content_embeddings.notes: HNSW vector index present but effectively unused at current scale — search ranking is keyword-first (see functions.search).

rls: SELECT public read — deliberately never tightened by 0081

columns: event_id text (NOT an FK — stable uuid-as-text or seed slug like 'family-fest-2026')
days jsonb (per-day RSVP map for Family Fest)
confirmed boolean DEFAULT true (0122 — false only when carried over unconfirmed from a finalized family-wide meeting poll)
rls: SELECT members-only (`auth.uid() is not null`) — tightened 0081 from public read; `events` itself stayed public

columns: id boolean PK DEFAULT true (singleton, CHECK id)
rls: SELECT public read (true) — DELIBERATE, it's the sign-in escape hatch, never gate this

columns: link_href/link_label text — ADDED 0083, DROPPED 0093 (migrated into links[0] first)
links jsonb DEFAULT '[]' — ordered [{href,label}] (0093, MULTI-LINK, replaces the single-link pair)
deadline_at timestamptz (0101 — distinct from starts_on/ends_on, an actual 'due by' reminder anchor)
signup_item_id text (0137 — a fest_schedule_items id, deep-links a 'Sign up' button)
home_callouts.notes: OBJECT DIMENSIONS: one optional cover photo (image_url) + a MULTI-link array (links, [{href,label}]) — same jsonb-array-of-link-objects shape later reused for fest_schedule_items.links (0142).

rls: ALL own-row (`user_id = auth.uid()`) — plain RLS, no RPC needed

columns: email text (UNIQUE on lower(email) where not null)
linked_user_id uuid -> profiles set null (auto-linked both directions)

functions.note: Grouped by domain. Every function not marked otherwise is `plpgsql` or `sql`, `SECURITY DEFINER`, `set search_path = ''`. GATE describes the runtime permission check inside the function body (separate from, and often stricter than, the Postgres-level GRANT EXECUTE, which is noted separately only when it differs from the default 'revoke all from public/anon, grant execute to authenticated').

identityAdmin.handle_new_user() [trigger, auth.users A: Seeds a profiles row from signup metadata (display_name, contact_email, invited_via) — SKIPS entirely if email not yet confirmed (OTP path defers to handle_user_email_confirmed).
identityAdmin.handle_user_email_confirmed() [trigger,: Creates/upserts the profiles row on first email verification, stamping joined_at.
identityAdmin.admin_members(): GATE: admin-only. Returns the full member directory incl. auth.users.email + house_id/house_name (widened over time; beta_tester column added 0029, removed 0100).
identityAdmin.set_admin(target, value): GATE: admin-only; additionally blocks removing your OWN admin access.
identityAdmin.delete_member(target): GATE: admin-only; blocks deleting yourself or another admin. Hard-deletes via `delete from auth.users` (cascades everywhere via FK). Storage files NOT cleaned up.
identityAdmin.recent_signins(limit_n default 50): GATE: admin-only. Reads auth.audit_log_entries, excludes token_refreshed/logout, caps at 200.
identityAdmin.admin_set_member_profile(target, patch j: GATE: admin-only AND admin_override.unlocked_until > now() (the two-admin-vote 'break glass' window). Writes only an explicit allow-list of profile fields — never id/is_admin/push prefs/email_alerts.
identityAdmin.request_admin_override() / admin_overrid: GATE: admin-only. Two-admin-vote flow to open/inspect/cancel the 24h admin_override.unlocked_until window (stale votes purged after 30 min).
identityAdmin.is_override_unlocked(): GATE: none in body — locked down entirely via GRANT (service_role ONLY, not even authenticated). The mac-mini calls this before writing a member's email via the GoTrue admin API.
identityAdmin.set_member_house(target, hid): GATE: admin-only. The ONLY write path to profiles.house_id (column itself is never client-writable).
identityAdmin.mark_notifications_seen() / mark_notific: GATE: implicit — all scoped to `recipient_id = auth.uid()`.
identityAdmin.send_broadcast_notification(title, body,: GATE: admin-only. audience in ('everyone','admins') — the original 'beta' option was dropped along with the Beta Tester concept (0100). Excludes anyone with event_attendance.status='not_going' on event_id when exclude_not_attending is true.
Internal fan-out primitive — GRANT: revoked from EVERY role including authenticated (callable only from other DEFINER functions). No-ops on self-notify or on a recipient's notif_types opt-out — EXCEPT type='help_urgent', which is hard-coded non-suppressible (0047).

committees.can_access_committee_area(cid, area): Central chat/meeting/search access predicate — see globalPatterns.
committees.request_to_join(cid, msg, requested_area: GATE: signed-in only; rejects the removed 'Committee Admin' role (mentions_removed_admin_role, 0076) and invalid areas (valid_committee_areas, 0073); no-ops if already a member via committee_members OR an already-linked roster slot (0090).
committees.review_join_request(req_id, approve): GATE: is_committee_lead(committee_id). On approve: upserts committee_members (preserving existing areas if the request carried none, 0075 bugfix) AND links/creates a committee_roster row, preferring an unlinked email match then a fuzzy name match (0090).
committees.set_committee_member(cid, target, is_mem: GATE: is_committee_lead(cid); a non-admin Lead cannot remove another Lead.
committees.set_committee_lead(cid, target, is_lead): GATE: is_committee_lead(cid) — widened in 0051 from admin-only to any existing Lead.
committees.set_committee_areas(cid, target, areas[]: GATE: is_committee_lead(cid) + valid_committee_areas + rejects the removed Admin role.
committees.set_my_committee_areas(cid, areas[]): GATE: signed-in AND already is_committee_member(cid) (self-service, no approval needed) — cannot self-appoint '· Lead'.
committees.leave_committee(cid): GATE: signed-in only, unrestricted otherwise (anyone including a Lead can remove themself). Also unlinks the caller's committee_roster row (0073 fix) so leaving actually revokes roster-gated chat access.
committees.valid_committee_areas(cid, areas[]): Pure validator (not an access gate) — rejects null/blank/'general' (case-insensitive); strips '· Lead' suffix before checking the committee_areas allow-list.
committees.mentions_removed_admin_role(areas[]): Pure validator — flags 'admin'/'admins'/'committee admin(s)' as an invalid, retired role.
committees.create_committee(name, emoji, descriptio: GATE: admin-only. Auto-slugs via slugify() + numeric-suffix dedup loop.
committees.update_committee(cid, name, emoji, descr: GATE: admin-only. Slug is immutable — never touched.
committees.add_committee_area(cid, area): GATE: admin-only. Upsert-style — re-adding a previously archived area un-archives it.
committees.rename_committee_area(cid, old, new): GATE: admin-only. Cascades through 7 places in ONE transaction: committee_areas, committee_roster.roles[], committee_members.areas[], committee_messages.area, committee_area_reads.area, committee_join_requests (both columns), and meetings.area (the 7th, added 0121).
committees.archive_committee_area(cid, area) / rest: GATE: admin-only. Does NOT strip the area from anyone's roles[] on archive.
committees.is_committee_area_archived(cid, area): Predicate — used as an INSERT guard so an archived committee/role's chat goes read-only (reads still work via the separate, untouched SELECT policy).

houses.set_house_rules(hid, rules): GATE: is_house_member(hid) — ANY house member (or admin) can overwrite the shared rules doc, last-write-wins, 20000-char cap. Bypasses the houses table's admin-only write RLS via SECURITY DEFINER.
houses.create_house_stay / update_house_stay /: GATE: create — is_house_member(house); update/delete — author OR admin.
houses._clean_guest_names(names[]): Pure helper (NOT SECURITY DEFINER, plain `language sql immutable`) — trims/caps names to 80 chars, drops blanks, caps list to 40.

workItems.create_work_item(title, notes, category,: GATE: signed-in only for MLR (house_id null); is_house_member(house_id) required for a house-scoped item. (Signature accreted across 0048->0049->0066->0069, each an explicit drop+recreate of the prior arity.)
workItems.update_work_item(id, title, notes, categ: GATE: admin-only through 0069; WIDENED in 0079 to admin OR the item's own created_by. House-move still requires admin OR is_house_member(new house).
workItems.mark_work_item_done(id): GATE: signed-in only (never admin-gated) + is_house_member(house) if the item is house-scoped. Stamps completed_by/completed_at (0088); only transitions status='open' rows.
workItems.sync_event_work_items(event_id, item_ids: GATE: admin-only. Idempotent full-replace of an event's linked work items.
workItems.add_work_item_to_event(event_id, item_id: GATE: signed-in only. Additive single link, `on conflict do nothing`.
workItems.notif_on_work_item_comment / notif_on_wo: Notify item creator + prior commenters / @mentioned member.

cabins.is_cabin_approver(cabin_id): Central access predicate for a specific place — see globalPatterns. Introduced 0114, retrofit as the new gate onto 5 pre-existing admin-only functions.
cabins.request_cabin_stay(cabin, check_in, chec: GATE: signed-in; booking-for-someone-else requires admin. Canonicalized in 0115 after two 7-arg overloads (room-picking 0092, notify-toggle 0108) silently coexisted and the client always hit 0092's (meaning notify:false never worked pre-0115).
cabins.review_cabin_stay(booking, approve, note: GATE: is_cabin_approver(cabin) — widened 0114 from admin-only. notify=false pre-stamps decision_email_sent_at so the mailer silently skips.
cabins.cancel_cabin_stay(booking, notify): GATE: booking's own user_id OR is_cabin_approver(cabin). Requester-cancels-own-booking OR notify=false both pre-stamp cancel_email_sent_at.
cabins.admin_update_cabin_booking(booking, chec: GATE: is_cabin_approver(cabin) — widened 0114 from admin-only. notify defaults FALSE (most edits are small corrections).
cabins.set_booking_rooms(booking, room_ids[]): GATE: booking's own user_id (self-service since 0106) OR is_cabin_approver(cabin) (widened 0114 from admin-only).
cabins.cabin_room_availability / cabin_availabi: GATE: none explicit (any authenticated caller); pure read computation, room-based math when the cabin has named rooms, else legacy cabin-wide room_count math. Now also accounts for beds (0111) and unassigned/room-less approved bookings (0108).
cabins.cabin_booking_notification(booking) / ca: GATE: none in body — locked to service_role ONLY via GRANT (the mac-mini's mailer/push senders).

help._help_recipients(eligible[], strict[], t: GATE: none client-facing — REVOKED from public/anon/AUTHENTICATED (internal-only, called only by other DEFINER functions). Filters on willing_to_help + notif_types + presence (event_attendance day-aware OR approved cabin_bookings) or all_willing.
help.request_help(description, category, ...,: GATE: signed-in; description/today required; <10 open requests; presence check UNLESS caller is admin (bypass added 0038) — the Beta Tester gate that existed 0037-0099 was removed in 0100.
help.respond_to_help(request, note) / withdra: GATE: signed-in; request must be status='open' for respond.
help.claim_help_item(item, claim): GATE: signed-in; race-safe conditional update (own claim or currently unclaimed); claiming also inserts a help_response.
help.set_help_status(request, status): GATE: the request's own user_id OR admin.
help.process_help_reminders(): GATE: none client-facing (revoked from all client roles) — pg_cron tick every minute for scheduled (not-yet-due-reminder) requests.

moderation.moderate_content_text() [trigger, posts/: Length caps + blocklist whole-word/phrase matching -> auto-sets status='pending'; a member changing status on UPDATE gets it silently pinned back (anti-self-unhide), except when the transaction-local GUC mlr.mod_bypass='1' is set (0128 — lets the moderation triggers themselves hold content without being reverted by this same guard).
moderation.apply_content_report() [trigger, content: At >=2 distinct reporters, auto-holds the parent post/comment.
moderation.hold_post_on_flagged_media() / hold_chat: Retroactively hold a post or chat message when its media_moderation verdict comes back flagged (chat posts optimistically, so this fires a few seconds after the message is already visible).
moderation.hold_content_on_media_verdict() [trigger: Fan-out version of the above across posts + both chat tables by matching storage_path.
moderation.report_content(entity_type, entity_id, r: GATE: signed-in only. Upserts caller's own report.
moderation.set_content_status(entity_type, entity_i: GATE: admin-only. status in ('visible','hidden'). Covers post/comment/committee_message/house_message (chat added 0128).
moderation.moderation_queue(): GATE: admin-only. Unified pending-or-reported queue across all 4 moderatable entity types.

broadcastsAndScheduling.alert_recipients(audience, event_id, exc: GATE: none in body — GRANT restricted to service_role ONLY. Signature grew twice (0015 zero-arg -> 0017 audience-arg -> 0127 event-targeting args), each an explicit DROP FUNCTION first to avoid overload collision.
broadcastsAndScheduling.schedule_broadcast(kind, payload, schedu: GATE: admin-only for all three; update/cancel are no-ops once the row has already fired.
broadcastsAndScheduling.run_scheduled_broadcasts(): GATE: none client-facing — REVOKED from public/anon/AUTHENTICATED entirely, runs ONLY as the pg_cron job owner. Fires due rows one at a time (per-row exception handling); honors excludeNotAttending, onlyUnconfirmed (0122), excludeCalloutDone (0102/0103 toggle), and showBanner (0126).

polls.create_poll(question, options[], closes_: GATE: ANY signed-in member (not admin-gated — the polls doctrine every later member-createable feature reuses).
polls.cast_poll_vote(poll, option): GATE: signed-in; poll must be open. One vote per member (PK-enforced), changeable any time.
polls.close_poll(poll) / delete_poll(poll): GATE: poll's own created_by OR admin.

meetings.can_organize_meeting(scope, committee_id: Central organize-gate — see globalPatterns. House and family scopes fall straight to admin-only.
meetings.create_meeting(scope, committee_id, area: GATE: can_organize_meeting(...). Scope grew to include 'family' in 0122. A short-lived p_email param (0117) was dropped again in the same-migration-range 0122 rework.
meetings.set_my_availability(meeting, answers jso: GATE: signed-in + meeting open + caller independently re-passes the room-access check (not trusting RLS). Bulk upserts the caller's OWN answers only.
meetings.finalize_meeting(meeting, slot, meet_url: GATE: meeting's created_by OR admin. Posts a join-link chat message into the room; notifies meeting_scheduled.
meetings.finalize_meeting_as_event(meeting, slot,: GATE: meeting's created_by OR admin. Creates a real events row (source='meeting'), carries Yes/If-need-be voters into event_attendance with confirmed=false, auto-queues a 14-day-before reminder with onlyUnconfirmed:true.
meetings.create_scheduled_meeting(scope, ..., sta: GATE: can_organize_meeting(...). One-shot — skips voting, immediately finalizes.
meetings.meeting_proposal_email(meeting) / meetin: GATE: none in body — service_role ONLY via GRANT. Recipient set widened over 3 migrations (0117 email_alerts-filtered members-only -> 0123 add account-less committee_roster/family_roster UNION branches -> 0132 drop the email_alerts filter entirely, since these are transactional, not opted-out-able -> 0133 re-restore the 0123 UNION branches that 0132 had accidentally dropped).

fest.can_edit_fest(): Central write-gate for all fest_* content — see globalPatterns. Redefined 0057 from committee_members-based to committee_roster.linked_user_id-based.
fest.sync_fest_lead_names() [trigger, profile: Rewrites the denormalized lead_name/chef_name snapshot on every fest row that person leads/cooks, matched ONLY by *_user_id (never free-text name) — an account-less lead is untouched.
fest._can_manage_schedule_signups(item) / _ca: The 0110 self-edit predicate, reused as the sign-up 'organizer' gate — see globalPatterns.
fest.sign_up_for_schedule_slot(item, slot, fo: GATE: signed-in only — the ORIGINAL 0135 version required _can_manage_schedule_signups to add someone else; 0136 DROPPED that organizer requirement entirely (any signed-in member may sign anyone up). 0143 added team support (shared team_id) and headcount mode (no slot dimension at all).
fest.remove_schedule_signup(signup): GATE: the row's OWN linked user, its adder (added_by, widened 0136), OR an organizer (_can_manage_schedule_signups).
fest.sign_up_for_activity_slot / remove_activ: Parallel, isolated mirror of the schedule-item versions for the legacy fest_activities path (0138) — same open-to-anyone sign-up gate, same self/adder/organizer removal gate.
fest.run_signup_reminders(): GATE: none client-facing (cron-only). Per-slot reminder push, dedup via fest_signup_reminders_sent.

tournaments.is_tournament_manager(t): Resolves the tournament's host (fest schedule item vs private activity, 0150) and defers to the matching manage-predicate — no separate organizer role.
tournaments.create_tournament(item, title, format, e: GATE: _can_manage_item_signups(item) (checked directly since no tournament exists yet to authorize against).
tournaments.update_tournament / delete_tournament: GATE: is_tournament_manager(t). Structural fields (e.g. bye_strategy) freeze once status <> 'setup'.
tournaments.import_entrants_from_signups(t): GATE: is_tournament_manager + status='setup'. Groups by fest_schedule_signups.team_id for teams; individuals -> pool.
tournaments.add_participant / remove_participant / a: GATE: is_tournament_manager throughout; remove_entrant blocked once status<>'setup'.
tournaments.generate_teams(t) / ungroup_teams(t): GATE: is_tournament_manager + status='setup' + entrant_type='team'. Uses pg_advisory_xact_lock(tournament_id) to serialize concurrent manager edits.
tournaments.generate_bracket(t, seed_order[]): GATE: is_tournament_manager + format='single_elim'. Fold-seeds, sizes to next power of two, auto-resolves byes, sets status='live', notifies tournament_published.
tournaments.generate_round_robin(t, seed_order[]): GATE: is_tournament_manager + format='round_robin'. Circle-method scheduling (phantom bye for odd counts); all matches immediately status='ready' (no propagation graph).
tournaments.generate_pools(t, pool_count, advance, s: GATE: is_tournament_manager + format='pools_bracket'. Snakes entrants into pools by seed, builds a round-robin per pool.
tournaments.generate_bracket_from_pools(t): GATE: is_tournament_manager + format='pools_bracket' + every pool match complete. Cross-seeds pool winners so they meet only late.
tournaments.record_match_result(match, winner, score: GATE: is_tournament_manager. Format-aware since 0145: single_elim/pools propagate via _tournament_advance + crown champion at the final; round_robin just records, crowns the standings leader once every game is complete. Winner alone is a complete result — scores are always optional. Ties only allowed for round_robin when allow_ties.
tournaments.clear_match_result / set_match_entrant /: GATE: is_tournament_manager throughout. set_match_entrant/clear cascade a recursive stale-downstream-result clear (_tournament_advance) when a decided match's composition changes.
tournaments.schedule_match(match, at, reminders[]) /: GATE: is_tournament_manager. Resets the fired-reminder ledger on reschedule.
tournaments.set_tournament_format(t, format): GATE: is_tournament_manager + status='setup' (must reset an already-generated bracket first). Added 0154 to un-freeze format choice.
tournaments.create_activity_tournament / import_entr: GATE: is_private_activity_host(activity). Idempotent since 0151 (returns the existing tournament rather than duplicating). Auto-seeds tournament_participants straight from the activity roster since 0152; individual-entrant_type tournaments get real tournament_entrants rows immediately (not just pool participants) since 0153.

privateActivities.create_private_activity(title, ..., memb: GATE: ANY signed-in member (no admin/role gate — mirrors the polls/work-items member-createable doctrine). Creator auto-added as host, RSVP'd going.
privateActivities.remove_private_activity_member: GATE: is_private_activity_host OR the member removing THEMSELF (self-leave allowed); the creator's own host row can never be removed by anyone.
privateActivities.set_private_activity_rsvp(activity, rsvp: GATE: is_private_activity_member(activity) — sets only the CALLER's own row.
privateActivities._notify_private_activity_invite(activity: Internal (owner-only GRANT) — the ONLY notification this feature ever sends, fired only when the organizer opts in ('🔔 Let them know').

search.search_conversations(query_embedding, qu: GATE: signed-in only; guests get nothing. Per-source-type visibility is RE-DERIVED inside the function from the CALLING member's auth.uid() (posts/comments: visible-or-own-or-admin; committee_message: can_access_committee_area + not-deleted + status check; house_message: is_house_member + same checks) — never trusts a pre-computed grant. Ranking evolved across 3 migrations: 0129 pure cosine similarity -> 0130 hybrid but with a ranking BUG (`ts_rank(...) > 0` leaked non-matches, since ts_rank returns a tiny non-zero epsilon even for non-matching multi-word queries) -> 0131 FIXED by filtering with the boolean `@@` operator instead, so a no-keyword-match query now correctly returns zero rows; embedding similarity is a tie-breaker ONLY among keyword-matched rows, never a standalone threshold.

events.create_event / update_event / delete_eve: GATE: admin-only throughout. delete_event also cleans up event_attendance rows by the same stable text id.
events.set_event_attendance(event, status, days: GATE: signed-in only; upserts ONLY the caller's own row. Stamps confirmed=true on every self-write (0122) — the mechanism by which 'reconfirming' a meeting-carried-over RSVP is just re-tapping the existing control. First-time transition into 'going' optionally broadcasts an opt-in 'event_rsvp' notification (0036).
events.clear_event_attendance(event): GATE: signed-in only; deletes only the caller's own row.

emailPools.all_member_recipients(): GATE evolved: 0028 admin-only -> 0031 admin OR any committee member -> 0123 also unions in account-less family_roster people.
emailPools.committee_member_recipients(cid): GATE evolved: 0028 is_committee_lead-only -> 0031 is_committee_member (any member) -> 0124 also returns each person's roles[] for the 'By Role' picker.
emailPools.directory_recipients(): GATE: any signed-in member (0031); 0123 also unions in account-less family_roster people.
emailPools.admin_recipients(): GATE: any signed-in member may call it (0124), but it only ever RETURNS admins — a targeting pool, not a privilege escalation.
emailPools.house_member_recipients(hid): GATE: is_house_member(hid) (0123). Unions account members + account-less family_roster people assigned to that house.

accessMatrix.legend: Per feature: who can READ, who can CREATE, who can EDIT (their own vs anyone's), who can ARCHIVE/DELETE. 'Member' = any signed-in profile. 'Guest' = unauthenticated browse.

posts.moderate: Admin (approve/hide via set_content_status); auto-hold at >=2 reports or a blocklist/AI hit

committeeChat.create: Same (message insert); blocked in an archived committee/role
committeeChat.moderate: Same status model as posts, extended in 0128

houseChat.read: Member of that house (profiles.house_id match) or admin

committees.read: Public (committee list); roster is members-only since 0081
committees.edit: Admin only (update_committee, rename_committee_area); Lead can set areas/lead status for their own committee
committees.archive/delete: Admin only, and it's always an archive (archived_at), never destructive

committeeMembership.join: Any member requests via request_to_join; Lead or admin approves
committeeMembership.selfServiceAreas: A current member can add/remove their OWN areas with no approval (set_my_committee_areas); cannot self-appoint Lead

houses.read: Public list of houses; membership (house_id) is admin-only to assign
houses.houseRules: ANY house member (or admin) can overwrite the shared rules text

houseStays.create: Any house member, own stay only
houseStays.edit/delete: Stay's own author, or admin, anytime

workItems.read: MLR items: any signed-in member; house items: that house's members (or admin)
workItems.create: Any signed-in member (house items require membership in that house)

cabins.read: Public place list; bookings visible to the requester or that place's approver/admin
cabins.create/edit places: Admin only (create_cabin, room CRUD, cabin details)
cabins.requestStay: Any signed-in member (admin can book on someone's behalf)
cabins.reviewRequests: Admin OR that place's designated approver (is_cabin_approver) — the approver need NOT be an app admin
cabins.messageGuests: Admin or that place's approver only

events.rsvp: Any signed-in member, own RSVP only
events.proposeDates(familyMeetingPoll): Admin only, open to every member to vote

helpRequests.create: Any signed-in member who is 'present' at the resort (admins bypass the presence check); urgent category bypasses willing/present targeting entirely and reaches everyone

polls (family).vote: Any signed-in member, one changeable vote

quickPollsInChat.vote: Any room member, own selection only
quickPollsInChat.seeWhoVoted: Nobody, if the poll is anonymous — enforced at the SQL/RLS layer, not just UI

meetings.read: Room members (committee/house scope) or any member (family scope)
meetings.propose: Admin (any room), or a committee Lead for their own committee; houses and family scope are admin-only
meetings.vote (availability): Any room member, own answers only

familyFestContent.editAll: Admin OR Family Fest committee member (can_edit_fest)
familyFestContent.editOwnItemOnly: A dinner/event/activity's assigned chef/lead/crew can edit that ONE item's operational details, even without full committee access

signUpsAndTeams.manage(config/slots): can_edit_fest() OR that item's own lead/crew
signUpsAndTeams.signSomeoneUp: ANY signed-in member (organizer gate was deliberately dropped, migration 0136) — can sign up self, another member, or a typed non-account name
signUpsAndTeams.removeSignup: The linked person, whoever added the row, or a manager

tournaments.read: Any signed-in member IF they can see the host activity/private-activity
tournaments.manage(seed/score/bracket): The host activity's lead/crew (or private-activity host), or admin — no separate tournament role

privateActivities.read: Creator, roster members, or admin ONLY — never public, never all-members
privateActivities.manage(roster/edit/archive): A host (creator or admin, or anyone promoted to role='host')
privateActivities.rsvp: Any roster member, own RSVP only
privateActivities.leave: Any roster member may remove themselves; the creator's own host row can never be removed

conversationSearch.read/query: Any signed-in member — results are silently scoped to exactly what that member could already see in Posts/committee chat/house chat; guests get nothing

broadcastsAndAlerts.activityTabNotification: Admin only, targeted Everyone or Admins

adminDashboard.access: profiles.is_admin only, enforced server-side via RLS/RPC gates everywhere — the UI gate (AdminGuard) is a courtesy, not the real wall
adminDashboard.mediaServerRestart: Narrower still — a single hardcoded owner email (lib/owner.ts), not the general is_admin flag

objectDimensions.note: Direct answer to 'what dimensions like photos/links exist on events or activities' — every media/photo/link/URL-bearing column found across all 158 migrations, by object.
objectDimensions.posts: Multiple photos/videos via post_media (storage_path + media_type image|video + position, ordered carousel) — NOT limited to one image. Legacy single-field post.image_path predates post_media and is superseded.
objectDimensions.committeeChatMessage / houseChatMessage: Multiple attachments via committee_message_media / house_message_media — media_type image|video|sticker|gif|file (the widest type set of any media table in the app, 0074 added 'file'), plus width/height (for aspect-ratio reservation) and file_name (for the 'file' type).
objectDimensions.workItem: Multiple photos/videos via work_item_media — media_type image|video ONLY (no sticker/gif/file, no width/height) — the simplest media shape in the app.
objectDimensions.resortEvent (events table): NO photo/media/link column at all — resort events carry only text/emoji/location, no image or URL field.
objectDimensions.festScheduleItemOrActivity (fest_schedul: ONE optional cover photo (image_url, plain text URL into the public site-assets bucket) PLUS a MULTI-link array (links jsonb, ordered [{href,label}] — introduced 0142 for schedule items, replacing an earlier single link_url/link_label pair from 0134; fest_activities never got the multi-link upgrade before being superseded by the 0141 merge). So: yes, an event/activity CAN carry a photo and link(s), but it's one photo + N links, not a gallery.
objectDimensions.homeCallout: ONE optional cover photo (image_url) PLUS a MULTI-link array (links jsonb, ordered [{href,label}], 0093) — the ORIGINAL precedent for the fest_schedule_items.links shape above. Also has deadline_at (a reminder anchor, not media) and signup_item_id (a deep-link reference to a fest_schedule_items row, not media either).
objectDimensions.meeting: ONE link field only — meet_url (a plain-text Google Meet URL), no photo.
objectDimensions.profile: ONE photo — avatar_url. Plus a full set of payment-app handles (venmo/zelle/cashapp/paypal/apple_cash) which are identifiers, not links/media.
objectDimensions.appImages / resortConfig: app_images.url is a single admin-managed site image per named key (e.g. the Home logo, Family Fest cover) — a small fixed set of site-wide images, not a per-object gallery.
objectDimensions.privateActivity / tournament / poll / he: NO photo/media/link columns on any of these objects themselves — purely text/structured data. (A private activity's tournament and a work item's comments are attached to objects that DO carry media elsewhere in the graph, but the rows themselves don't.)
